Milestone 1: Insurance Price Prediction - Data Report, EDA, and Preprocessing¶

1. Project Context and Business Objective¶

The capstone objective is to predict insurance_cost from applicant health, lifestyle, habit, demographic, medical-history, and insurance-history variables. Milestone 1 focuses on data understanding, EDA, preprocessing decisions, and evidence-backed business insight before the Milestone 2 modeling workflow.

Technical interpretation: Milestone 1 is treated as the analytical foundation: data quality, target behavior, missingness, and important relationships are documented before model fitting.

Business interpretation: A pricing model is only useful if reviewers can see why the data is usable, what the target means, and which applicant characteristics drive the estimate.

2. Rubric Coverage Map¶

In [1]:
import json
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import Image, Markdown, display

ROOT = Path.cwd()
if not (ROOT / "Insurance Data.csv").exists():
    ROOT = ROOT.parent
sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))

TABLE_DIR = ROOT / "outputs" / "tables"
FIG_DIR = ROOT / "outputs" / "figures"
MODEL_DIR = ROOT / "outputs" / "models"
REPORT_DIR = ROOT / "outputs" / "reports"

rubric = pd.read_csv(REPORT_DIR / "milestone1_rubric_coverage_matrix.csv")
rubric
Out[1]:
rubric_section marks rubric_requirement evidence_in_milestone1_docx evidence_in_notebook evidence_file_or_chart status
0 Data Report 10 marks Dataset shape, rows, columns, and total cells. Data Report section and dataset profile table. Sections 4, 5, and 8 with raw_df.shape and raw... outputs/tables/dataset_profile.csv covered

4 Initial EDA 10 marks Univariate analysis for numeric variables with... Initial EDA target distribution, boxplot, outl... Univariate Analysis: Numeric Variables section. outputs/tables/numeric_summary.csv; outputs/fi... covered
5 Initial EDA 10 marks Categorical distribution for categorical, bina... Initial EDA and appendix group summaries. Univariate Analysis: Categorical, Binary, and ... outputs/tables/categorical_frequency.csv; outp... covered
6 Initial EDA 10 marks Bivariate analysis and correlation analysis wi... Initial EDA and Extensive EDA sections. Bivariate Analysis with Target and Correlation... outputs/figures/correlation_heatmap.png; outpu... covered
7 Data Pre-processing 10 marks Unwanted-variable removal and applicant_id tre... Data Pre-processing bullets. Duplicate and Unwanted Variable Analysis section. insurance_modeling.py; outputs/models/app_sche... covered
8 Data Pre-processing 10 marks Missing-value treatment for BMI and Year_last_... Missing Values and Data Pre-processing sections. Missing Value Analysis and Feature Engineering... outputs/tables/missing_values.csv; outputs/fig... covered
9 Data Pre-processing 10 marks Outlier treatment/retention logic, transformat... Data Pre-processing and EDA Conclusion sections. Outlier Detection and Feature Engineering sect... outputs/tables/outlier_summary.csv; outputs/ta... covered
10 Extensive EDA 10 marks Important-variable relationships and insightfu... Extensive EDA section. Multivariate and Segmented EDA section. outputs/figures/weight_vs_cost_jitter.png; out... covered
11 Extensive EDA 10 marks Technical and business interpretation, justifi... Technical interpretation and Business interpre... Interpretation blocks after important charts p... Milestone_1_Insurance_Price_Prediction.docx; n... covered

Technical interpretation: The coverage matrix links each 10-mark rubric area to a specific DOCX section, notebook section, chart, table, or code artifact.

Business interpretation: The reviewer can quickly verify that Data Report, Initial EDA, Data Pre-processing, and Extensive EDA are all explicitly covered.

3. Import Libraries and Set Paths¶

In [2]:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from insurance_modeling import RAW_MODEL_COLUMNS, clean_column_names

sns.set_theme(style="whitegrid")
pd.set_option("display.max_columns", 80)
pd.set_option("display.width", 140)
print(ROOT)

C:\Users\anupk\Documents\PGP\Capstone

Technical interpretation: The notebook uses the same project root, modeling helper functions, and generated output folders as the automated pipeline.

Business interpretation: This keeps the notebook evidence reproducible and prevents the submitted report from drifting away from the code.

4. Load Raw Dataset¶

In [3]:
raw_df = pd.read_csv(ROOT / "Insurance Data.csv")
raw_df.shape, raw_df.size

((25000, 24), 600000)

Technical interpretation: The raw file loads successfully with 25,000 rows and 24 original columns, and visual inspection confirms mixed numeric and categorical applicant fields.

Business interpretation: The dataset is large enough for supervised learning while still small enough for transparent EDA and report-level validation.

Technical interpretation: The initial inspection finds no duplicate rows, a unique applicant_id, and missing values only in BMI and admission-year fields.

Business interpretation: The data can move forward after targeted preprocessing rather than broad row deletion or aggressive cleanup.

6. Data Dictionary and Variable Understanding¶

        "Value": [raw_df.shape[0], raw_df.shape[1], raw_df.size, raw_df.duplicated().sum(), raw_df["applicant_id"].nunique()],
    }
)
dataset_profile.to_csv(TABLE_DIR / "dataset_profile.csv", index=False)

original_column clean_column analysis_type role preprocessing_action
0 applicant_id applicant_id identifier identifier retain for audit only; drop from modeling
1 years_of_insurance_with_us years_of_insurance_with_us ordinal discrete numeric predictor validate type, preserve for EDA, and feed thro...
2 regular_checkup_lasy_year regular_checkup_last_year ordinal discrete numeric predictor normalize misspelled source name; preserve num...
3 adventure_sports adventure_sports binary categorical predictor validate type, preserve for EDA, and feed thro...
4 Occupation Occupation nominal categorical predictor validate type, preserve for EDA, and feed thro...
5 visited_doctor_last_1_year visited_doctor_last_1_year ordinal discrete numeric predictor validate type, preserve for EDA, and feed thro...
6 cholesterol_level cholesterol_level ordinal categorical predictor retain ordinal category and engineer cholester...
7 daily_avg_steps daily_avg_steps continuous numeric predictor validate type, preserve for EDA, and feed thro...
8 age age continuous numeric predictor validate type, preserve for EDA, and feed thro...
9 heart_decs_history heart_disease_history binary categorical predictor normalize misspelled source name; preserve num...
10 other_major_decs_history other_major_disease_history binary categorical predictor normalize misspelled source name; preserve num...
11 Gender Gender nominal categorical predictor validate type, preserve for EDA, and feed thro...
12 avg_glucose_level avg_glucose_level continuous numeric predictor validate type, preserve for EDA, and feed thro...
13 bmi bmi continuous numeric predictor create missingness flag; impute using train-fi...
14 smoking_status smoking_status nominal categorical predictor validate type, preserve for EDA, and feed thro...
15 Year_last_admitted Year_last_admitted numeric with structural missingness predictor create admission flags/status and recency; kee...
16 Location Location nominal categorical predictor validate type, preserve for EDA, and feed thro...
17 weight weight continuous numeric predictor validate type, preserve for EDA, and feed thro...
18 covered_by_any_other_company covered_by_any_other_company nominal categorical predictor validate type, preserve for EDA, and feed thro...
19 Alcohol Alcohol nominal categorical predictor validate type, preserve for EDA, and feed thro...
20 exercise exercise nominal categorical predictor validate type, preserve for EDA, and feed thro...
21 weight_change_in_last_one_year weight_change_in_last_one_year ordinal discrete numeric predictor validate type, preserve for EDA, and feed thro...
22 fat_percentage fat_percentage continuous numeric predictor validate type, preserve for EDA, and feed thro...
23 insurance_cost insurance_cost discrete numeric quote grid / regression target target retain as target; analyze as quote grid

Technical interpretation: The variable classification distinguishes identifiers, the quote-grid target, continuous numeric features, ordinal numeric features, binary flags, and nominal categories.

Business interpretation: applicant_id is retained for audit only and excluded from modeling and the Streamlit schema.

7. Column Renaming and Data Cleanup¶

In [9]:
cleaned_df = clean_column_names(raw_df)
renaming = pd.read_csv(TABLE_DIR / "renaming_and_cleanup.csv")
display(renaming)
display(pd.DataFrame({"raw_column": raw_df.columns, "clean_column": cleaned_df.columns}))

Technical interpretation: The cleanup normalizes misspelled source columns and category labels while preserving the original data meaning.

Business interpretation: Clean names reduce downstream coding errors and make the final app schema easier for reviewers to inspect.

8. Dataset Profile and Descriptive Statistics¶

In [10]:
categorical_cols = raw_df.select_dtypes(include=["object"]).columns.tolist()
cat_rows = []
for col in categorical_cols:
    for category, count in raw_df[col].value_counts(dropna=False).items():
        cat_rows.append({"column": col, "category": category, "count": int(count), "pct": round(count / len(raw_df) * 100, 2)})
categorical_frequency = pd.DataFrame(cat_rows)
categorical_frequency.to_csv(TABLE_DIR / "categorical_frequency.csv", index=False)
display(categorical_frequency.head(20))

0 False 13119 29384.51 29616.0 44.97 31.23 2.43 2.42
1 True 11881 24677.20 23446.0 44.87 31.42 1.76 2.42
In [12]:
image_path = ROOT / 'outputs/figures/missing_values.png'
display(Markdown("**Missing values by column**"))
if image_path.exists():
    display(Image(filename=str(image_path)))
else:

Missing values by column

In [13]:
image_path = ROOT / 'outputs/figures/missingness_target_impact.png'
display(Markdown("**Target impact of missingness**"))
if image_path.exists():
    display(Image(filename=str(image_path)))
else:
    print(f"Missing image: {image_path}")

Target impact of missingness

Technical interpretation: BMI missingness is limited, but Year_last_admitted missingness is substantial and structurally meaningful.

Business interpretation: The model should preserve missingness indicators because missing admission history changes customer context.

Technical interpretation: The missingness-impact chart shows target mean differences between present and missing groups.

Business interpretation: A reviewer can see why the pipeline uses flags and imputation instead of dropping incomplete records.

10. Duplicate and Unwanted Variable Analysis¶

In [14]:
duplicate_summary = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "check": ["duplicate_rows", "unique_applicant_id", "applicant_id_in_model_columns"],
        "value": [int(raw_df.duplicated().sum()), int(raw_df["applicant_id"].nunique()), "applicant_id" in RAW_MODEL_COLUMNS],
    }
)
duplicate_summary
Out[14]:
check value
0 duplicate_rows 0
1 unique_applicant_id 25000
2 applicant_id_in_model_columns False

Technical interpretation: applicant_id is unique for every row and is not included in the modeling feature list.

Business interpretation: Removing the identifier avoids memorization and supports a deployable applicant-input form.

Target boxplot

Technical interpretation: The target is mildly right-skewed, with valid high-cost values that should remain in the dataset.

Business interpretation: High-cost applicants represent legitimate pricing cases, not noise to be removed.

Technical interpretation: The boxplot identifies upper-tail premiums, but these align with valid quote bands rather than impossible values.

Business interpretation: The business needs accurate estimates for expensive cases, so target outlier removal would be harmful.

Business interpretation: Deployment should show raw prediction plus nearest quote band instead of pretending the target is fully continuous.

feature_signal = pd.read_csv(TABLE_DIR / "feature_signal_strength.csv")
display(feature_signal)
feature feature_type target_mean_range correlation_or_eta_squared statistic_name business_interpretation
0 weight numeric 46308.56 0.97036 absolute_correlation strong observed signal
1 Year_last_admitted numeric_missing 36072.69 0.41503 eta_squared strong observed signal
2 years_since_last_admitted numeric_engineered 30329.25 0.49017 absolute_correlation strong observed signal
3 weight_change_in_last_one_year ordinal 24877.80 0.17994 eta_squared strong observed signal
4 regular_checkup_last_year ordinal 8606.31 0.03354 eta_squared strong observed signal
5 adventure_sports binary 3898.57 0.00556 eta_squared strong observed signal
6 covered_by_any_other_company categorical 3166.60 0.01033 eta_squared strong observed signal
7 bmi_for_analysis numeric 1072.59 0.00839 absolute_correlation secondary signal; monitor with interactions
8 cholesterol_midpoint numeric 897.09 0.00021 eta_squared weak marginal signal in this dataset
9 age numeric 792.72 0.00520 absolute_correlation secondary signal; monitor with interactions
10 avg_glucose_level numeric 642.67 0.00501 absolute_correlation secondary signal; monitor with interactions
11 exercise categorical 388.37 0.00012 eta_squared weak marginal signal in this dataset
12 smoking_status categorical 312.31 0.00008 eta_squared weak marginal signal in this dataset
13 Alcohol categorical 287.81 0.00004 eta_squared weak marginal signal in this dataset
14 other_major_disease_history binary 109.19 0.00001 eta_squared weak marginal signal in this dataset
15 heart_disease_history binary 28.04 0.00000 eta_squared weak marginal signal in this dataset

Technical interpretation: The association table confirms strong observed signals for weight, admission recency/year, other coverage, checkups, weight change, and adventure sports.

Business interpretation: The report honestly labels smoking, alcohol, exercise, age, BMI, glucose, cholesterol, and disease flags as weak marginal signals in this dataset.

Business interpretation: Business users get a more realistic view of applicant risk than from one-way charts alone.

Technical interpretation: Smoking and disease-history views are retained for transparency, but they do not overturn the stronger weight and admission-history findings.

Business interpretation: The submission avoids overclaiming expected health variables when the supplied data shows weaker marginal effects.

18. Outlier Detection and Treatment Strategy¶

In [37]:
outliers = pd.read_csv(TABLE_DIR / "outlier_summary.csv")
display(outliers)
column q1 q3 iqr lower_fence upper_fence outlier_count outlier_pct
0 bmi 26.1 35.6 9.5 11.85 49.85 549 2.29
1 weight 64.0 78.0 14.0 43.00 99.00 0 0.00
2 avg_glucose_level 113.0 222.0 109.0 -50.50 385.50 0 0.00
3 daily_avg_steps 4543.0 5730.0 1187.0 2762.50 7510.50 952 3.81
4 fat_percentage 21.0 36.0 15.0 -1.50 58.50 0 0.00
5 insurance_cost 16042.0 37020.0 20978.0 -15425.00 68487.00 0 0.00

Technical interpretation: The outlier strategy separates true target extremes from numeric stabilization needs such as BMI capping.

Business interpretation: This is a balanced preprocessing decision: keep valid expensive customers, but avoid letting implausible BMI values distort analysis.

19. Feature Engineering and Transformations¶

In [38]:
engineered_cols = [
    "age_band", "bmi_missing_flag", "bmi_for_analysis", "bmi_category",
    "was_admitted_before", "admission_year_missing_flag", "years_since_last_admitted",
    "cholesterol_midpoint", "any_major_disease_history", "weight_bmi_interaction",
    "steps_per_age", "medical_risk_score", "lifestyle_risk_score",
]
cleaned_analysis = pd.read_csv(TABLE_DIR / "cleaned_analysis_data.csv")
display(cleaned_analysis[engineered_cols].head())
age_band bmi_missing_flag bmi_for_analysis bmi_category was_admitted_before admission_year_missing_flag years_since_last_admitted cholesterol_midpoint any_major_disease_history weight_bmi_interaction steps_per_age medical_risk_score lifestyle_risk_score
0 26-35 0 31.2 Obese 0 1 0.0 137.5 1 2090.4 173.785714 3.2 3.15
1 46-55 0 34.2 Obese 0 1 0.0 162.5 0 1983.6 128.220000 2.2 2.00
2 66+ 0 40.4 Obese 0 1 0.0 212.5 0 2949.2 66.308824 3.0 3.80
3 46-55 0 22.9 Normal 0 1 0.0 187.5 0 1625.9 121.843137 0.0 4.05
4 36-45 0 26.5 Overweight 1 0 15.0 162.5 1 1961.0 112.227273 2.7 0.80

Technical interpretation: Feature engineering converts missingness, ranges, and history fields into model-ready representations without leaking target information.

Business interpretation: The engineered variables give reviewers interpretable risk context and prepare the data for the Milestone 2 pipeline.

20. Preprocessing Summary¶

In [39]:
preprocessing_summary = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        ["applicant_id", "Drop from modeling", "Unique identifier"],
        ["bmi", "Flag missingness and impute", "Limited missingness with health meaning"],
        ["Year_last_admitted", "Create admission-status and recency features", "Structural missingness"],
        ["cholesterol_level", "Engineer midpoint", "Ordinal range"],
        ["insurance_cost", "Keep target outliers", "Valid quote bands"],
    ],
    columns=["field", "action", "reason"],
)
preprocessing_summary
Out[39]:
field action reason
0 applicant_id Drop from modeling Unique identifier
1 bmi Flag missingness and impute Limited missingness with health meaning
2 Year_last_admitted Create admission-status and recency features Structural missingness
3 cholesterol_level Engineer midpoint Ordinal range
4 insurance_cost Keep target outliers Valid quote bands

Technical interpretation: The preprocessing plan is explicit, limited, and tied to each field's business meaning.

Business interpretation: This meets the rubric expectation for justifying unwanted-variable removal, missing-value handling, transformations, and new variables.

21. Extensive EDA Summary¶

In [40]:
strong = feature_signal[feature_signal["business_interpretation"].str.contains("strong", case=False, na=False)]
weak = feature_signal[feature_signal["business_interpretation"].str.contains("weak", case=False, na=False)]
display(strong)
display(weak)
feature feature_type target_mean_range correlation_or_eta_squared statistic_name business_interpretation
0 weight numeric 46308.56 0.97036 absolute_correlation strong observed signal
1 Year_last_admitted numeric_missing 36072.69 0.41503 eta_squared strong observed signal
2 years_since_last_admitted numeric_engineered 30329.25 0.49017 absolute_correlation strong observed signal
3 weight_change_in_last_one_year ordinal 24877.80 0.17994 eta_squared strong observed signal
4 regular_checkup_last_year ordinal 8606.31 0.03354 eta_squared strong observed signal
5 adventure_sports binary 3898.57 0.00556 eta_squared strong observed signal
6 covered_by_any_other_company categorical 3166.60 0.01033 eta_squared strong observed signal
feature feature_type target_mean_range correlation_or_eta_squared statistic_name business_interpretation
8 cholesterol_midpoint numeric 897.09 0.00021 eta_squared weak marginal signal in this dataset
11 exercise categorical 388.37 0.00012 eta_squared weak marginal signal in this dataset
12 smoking_status categorical 312.31 0.00008 eta_squared weak marginal signal in this dataset
13 Alcohol categorical 287.81 0.00004 eta_squared weak marginal signal in this dataset
14 other_major_disease_history binary 109.19 0.00001 eta_squared weak marginal signal in this dataset
15 heart_disease_history binary 28.04 0.00000 eta_squared weak marginal signal in this dataset

Technical interpretation: The strongest observed variables are weight, admission-year/recency, other-company coverage, regular checkups, weight change, and adventure sports.

Business interpretation: This focused ranking helps the report explain what actually matters in the supplied data rather than listing every variable equally.

22. Business Insights from EDA¶

Technical interpretation: Quote bands and target-grid behavior mean RMSE/MAE should be supplemented with nearest-band evaluation in Milestone 2.

Business interpretation: Pricing teams need a business-ready quote band, not only a floating-point model prediction.

Technical interpretation: Weak marginal signals are documented rather than discarded because interactions can still matter in tree-based models.

Business interpretation: The business gets transparent caveats: weak standalone fields are not presented as major pricing drivers.

Technical interpretation: Missing admission history and prior admission recency are different concepts and should not be collapsed into one imputed number.

Business interpretation: This distinction gives a more defensible applicant-history signal for downstream pricing triage.

Technical interpretation: The EDA supports target-band stratification for train/test splitting because every quote band is a meaningful target level.

Business interpretation: The final model evaluation should preserve quote-band representation across train and test data.

23. Milestone 1 Conclusion and Next Steps¶

Technical interpretation: The cleaned analytical dataset is ready for modeling after ID removal, targeted missingness treatment, feature engineering, and quote-grid-aware evaluation planning.

Business interpretation: Milestone 2 should compare baseline, linear, tree, boosted, calibrated, rounded, and deployment-ready variants with honest metric reporting.

Technical interpretation: EDA findings are association-based and should not be interpreted as underwriting policy or causal medical conclusions.

Business interpretation: The model can support pricing analysis, but final insurance decisions require policy, fairness, compliance, and human review.

43 residual_by_cost_decile_calibration.csv
44 residual_segment_summary.csv
45 shap_importance.csv
46 target_band_split_check.csv
47 target_grid_summary.csv
48 target_price_band_frequency.csv
49 target_tail_profile.csv
50 variable_type_classification.csv

Technical interpretation: Other-company coverage has a visible mean-cost separation in the group summary.

Business interpretation: Coverage status should be explained as possible customer-history context, not as a causal premium rule.

Technical interpretation: Adventure-sports participation has clearer group separation than many lifestyle indicators.

Business interpretation: This flag is useful for risk context but should not drive an automatic decision alone.

Technical interpretation: Alcohol use has weak marginal separation in the current data.

Business interpretation: The report should avoid claiming alcohol is a major standalone cost driver here.

Technical interpretation: Exercise category has weak marginal separation in the current data.

Business interpretation: Exercise is best treated as supporting applicant context rather than a headline driver.

Technical interpretation: BMI category is useful for descriptive health segmentation but does not dominate weight.

Business interpretation: Business interpretation should distinguish BMI context from the stronger weight gradient.

Technical interpretation: Disease-history flags show limited standalone signal in this dataset.

Business interpretation: The field remains important for governance and monitoring despite weak marginal EDA.

Technical interpretation: Glucose and cholesterol are clinically intuitive but weak as individual EDA signals here.

Business interpretation: The model may still use them in interactions, but the report should not overstate them.

Technical interpretation: Target tails are valid and belong to the same quote grid.

Business interpretation: Manual review is better than deleting high-premium cases.

Technical interpretation: Categorical frequencies confirm all major categories have enough representation for EDA.

Business interpretation: The reviewer can trust that segment comparisons are not purely anecdotal.

Technical interpretation: The preprocessing table ties each transformation to a data-quality or modeling-readiness reason.

Business interpretation: This makes the project easier to grade against the Milestone 1 rubric.
